hints.question: Do I have to solve each maze separately?
hints.answer: No. You write one algorithm that works for any maze, and the same code runs on every scenario.

hints.question: How do I get my code to run for every step of the maze?
hints.answer: Wrap your logic in the repeat loop. It runs your code over and over until you reach the end, so you only need to describe what to do for a single step.

hints.question: How do I make sure only one of my checks happens each step?
hints.answer: Use a single `if` / `else if` / `else` chain rather than several separate `if`s. That way exactly one branch runs each time through the loop.

hints.question: I turn, but the character gets stuck in the same spot.
hints.answer: Turning only changes the direction the character faces, it doesn't take a step. After turning, you still need to `move()`.

hints.question: How do I turn around at a dead end?
hints.answer: There's no turn-around function. Turning the same way twice (two left turns, or two right turns) leaves the character facing back the way it came.
